companyDescription:
Sensor Försäkring är en försäkringsagentur som erbjuder Person- och Hälsoförsäkringar i Sverige. Dessutom försäkrar vi dina saker, om du vill det.

Försäkringar upplevs av många som ganska svåra att förstå. Ofta har vi dålig koll på vilket skydd vi har och att ta reda på vilket skydd vi har, eller borde ha, upplevs generellt som ganska krångligt. Vi vill vara alternativet som erbjuder produkter som från början passar det liv våra kunder lever och vi vill säkerställa att alla våra kunder får det skydd de behöver, genom individuell rådgivning utifrån varje kunds unika situation.

Vårt löfte till våra kunder är att våra försäkringspaket ska vara prisvärda, lätta att förstå och ge den hjälp du behöver när olyckan är framme. Vi ska vara enkla att ha att göra med oavsett hur och i vilket ärende du kontaktar oss samt att vi ska ge dig professionell och snabb hjälp, när du väl behöver den.
companySlogan: Försäkringar du kan förstå, utformade efter dina behov. Vi skyddar dig och din familjs ekonomi
descriptionHtml:
Som analaytiker inom företagsförsäkring får du en central roll i vår prissättningsstrategi, riskanalys och lönsamhetsuppföljning. Du arbetar nära underwriters, produktansvariga och dataanalytiker och bidrar med aktuariell expertis i hela värdekedjan – från produktutveckling till portföljstyrning.


Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:

  • Utveckla och underhålla prismodeller för företagsförsäkringar
  • Genomföra reservsättningar och lönsamhetsanalyser
  • Analysera trender i skadeutfall och riskexponering
  • Stödja affärsområden med aktuariell rådgivning vid produktutveckling, specialuppdrag och förnyelsearbete.
  • Delta i intern och extern rapportering, inklusive Solvens II och andra regulatoriska krav


Kvalifikationer

  • Akademisk examen i matematik, statistik, aktuarieprogram eller motsvarande
  • Några års erfarenhet som aktuarie, gärna inom sakförsäkring och särskilt företagsförsäkring och gruppaffär.
  • Har goda kunskaper i Excel och gärna programmeringsvana (t.ex. R, Python eller SAS)
  • Har lätt att kommunicera och arbeta med kollegor från alla avdelningar


Vi erbjuder:

  • En stimulerande roll med stor möjlighet att påverka
  • Ett modernt kontor med flexibilitet att arbeta delvis på distans
  • Ett varmt och inkluderande team med hög kompetens och gott samarbete


Vi vill att du ska lockas av att vi är en uppstickare på försäkringsmarknaden med allt vad det innebär. Du är prestigelös och beredd på att hugga i där det behövs med inställningen att vi gör det för företagets bästa, en förutsättning är att du trivs i en föränderlig miljö med korta beslutsvägar. För rätt person är det här en spännande resa som bara har tagit sin början, känslan av delaktighet och påverkan är enorm och i takt med företagets tillväxt finns även stora möjligheter för vår personal att växa.


Vid frågor är du välkommen att ta kontakt med Tommy Grape på tommy.grape@sensorgroup.se eller 0703957711. Urval sker löpande, så tveka inte, sök redan idag!


Sensor Försäkring är en nytänkande utmanare på svenska försäkringsmarknaden. Vi är idag ca 200 anställda i Luleå, Umeå, Stockholm och planerar tillväxt inom samtliga avdelningar det närmaste året. Alla anställda ingår i ett långsiktigt utbildningsprogram som syftar till att säkerställa att du får tillräckligt med verktyg för att lyckas i din yrkesroll samt att du över tid får möjlighet att utvecklas och ta dig an större och mer komplexa utmaningar hos oss.

companyDescription:
Baker Tilly Stockholm is an Auditing, Accounting and Payroll firm that offers customized solutions and advices. 
Thanks to our Baker Tilly International network, which is present in more than 140 countries, we can also offer and assist with issues and expertise that extend beyond the Swedish borders. 
You will find us in our nice premises at Luntmakargatan 46 in central Stockholm. 

More about us:
Today we are about 35 employees, but we are continuously expanding with new employees and customers. 
Our goal is to grow by 15% per year over the next five years. 
We are a familiar company with a good atmosphere, laughter in the corridors and a good corporate culture, which we are proud of. 

In our Swedish network Baker Tilly Sweden, we currently have around 800 employees at about 80 offices from Trelleborg in the south to Luleå in the north. 
What sets us apart at Baker Tilly Stockholm is how we give entrepreneurial companies, regardless of size, the best of both worlds – the sense of service and personal commitment of a locally owned agency combined with the resources and specialist expertise of the Baker Tilly Network. This makes us unique and it gives our customers the security and competence that they need!
companySlogan: Audit, Accounting, Advisory and Payroll at Luntmakargatan 46 in Stockholm City Center
descriptionHtml:
Vilka är vi

Baker Tilly Stockholm är en revisions-, redovisnings- och rådgivningsbyrå som sitter i trevliga lokaler i centrala Stockholm och är idag ca 34 anställda. Vi expanderar och söker löpande medarbetare. Vi är ett familjärt gäng som gillar att ha kul tillsammans och på jobbet!

Vad vi erbjuder

Vi erbjuder dig en utvecklande roll där du får arbeta med många olika kunder, i olika branscher. Vi arbetar efter våra värdeord; Drivna, Tillgängliga och Intresserade vilket innebär att vi lägger stort fokus på rådgivning med personligt engagemang och proaktivitet gentemot våra kunder.

Vi erbjuder en personlig utbildningsplanering kombinerat med en individuell karriärsutvecklingsplan. Du får också möjligheter att vara delaktig i byråns framtida utveckling.

Om rollen

Som revisor arbetar du i kunduppdrag med att granska företags redovisning och förvaltning. Du har direktkontakt med kund och har därmed ett stort utrymme till eget ansvar.

För att lyckas i rollen har du,

  • Minst en kandidatexamen om 180hp inom ekonomi.
  • 2-3 års arbetslivserfarenhet av revisionsarbete.
  • Ett stort intresse av att vilja utvecklas inom revision och rådgivning.

Personliga egenskaper

Som person är du ansvarstagande och van att arbeta självständigt. Du har ett driv och en vilja att utvecklas i din roll. Du är tydlig i din kommunikation och känner dig bekväm med att hantera både interna och externa kontakter. Självklart är din samarbetsförmåga mycket god och du är van att jobba mot deadlines. Du har en förmåga att behålla lugnet även i stressiga situationer. Du har ett affärsmässigt tänkande med intresset och viljan att leverera lösningar och hjälpa våra kunder att bli lönsamma.

I denna rekrytering lägger vi stor vikt vid din personlighet.

Skicka in din ansökan redan idag då vi arbetar med löpande urval.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

companyDescription: Vi är en statlig myndighet som arbetar med frågor som rör Sveriges försvars-, säkerhets- eller utrikespolitik.
companySlogan: Vi arbetar med frågor som rör Sveriges utrikes-, säkerhets- och försvarspolitik.
descriptionHtml:
Vill du arbeta på en växande myndighet med ett samhällsviktigt uppdrag? Drivs du av komplexa frågor och utmanande utredningsarbeten? Trivs du med att vara ett analytiskt stöd till andra? Då ska du söka den här rollen som bolagsanalytiker på enheten för utländska direktinvesteringar (UDI-enheten).

Arbetsbeskrivning Du ger analytiskt stöd i ärenden rörande granskning av utländska direktinvesteringar inom skyddsvärda områden. Det analytiska stödet består bland annat i att utreda och kartlägga komplexa ägarstrukturer samt analysera inflytande på olika nivåer. Du deltar i arbetet med att fortsatt utveckla ISP:s analys- och utredningsarbete under genomförandet av myndighetens uppdrag som Sveriges myndighet för granskning av utländska direktinvesteringar.

Kvalifikationer

  • Akademisk examen inom relevant område från högskola/universitet eller motsvarande kompetens förvärvad på annat sätt och som arbetsgivaren bedömer likvärdig
  • Minst tre års relevant och dokumenterad erfarenhet av kvalificerat analys- eller utredningsarbete, till exempel genom arbete inom bekämpning av ekonomisk brottslighet, utredning av komplexa ägarstrukturer, avancerat AML/KYC-arbete, omfattande bakgrundskontroller eller liknande
  • Aktuell, flerårig och relevant arbetslivserfarenhet av att inhämta, bearbeta, tolka och analysera information
  • Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka sig på engelska och svenska i tal och skrift

Meriterande

  • Relevant erfarenhet av att granska, hitta och analysera information från stora mängder ostrukturerad data från hemsidor eller datakällor
  • Kunskaper i flera språk
  • Utredningserfarenhet av och kunskap om inflytande och ägande i företag, bulvanförhållanden samt utländska associationsformer

Vi söker dig som har förmåga att arbeta självständigt och som samtidigt tar ansvar för helheten och för verksamhetens bästa. Du är analytisk, kreativ, noggrann och har erfarenhet av och förmåga att arbeta under tidspress och ändå leverera inom utsatta tidsramar. Vidare har du förmåga att arbeta strukturerat i komplexa sammanhang för att kunna prioritera och avgränsa ditt arbete utifrån uppdraget. Arbetsuppgifterna ställer krav på samarbetsförmåga och förutsätter att du har en positiv inställning till att samarbeta med olika yrkesgrupper såväl internt som externt. För att trivas i rollen behöver du ha ett högt sekretessmedvetande och ett kritiskt och sakligt förhållningssätt till ditt arbete. Du kan hantera och analysera omfattande informationsmängder samt avgränsa och förmedla komplex information. Vi förutsätter att du har intresse för utrikes-, säkerhets- och försvarspolitik. Vi fäster stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper.

Arbetsgruppen Som bolagsanalytiker är du placerad på Enheten för utländska direktinvesteringar. Du kommer till en arbetsgrupp med entusiastiska och kompetenta kollegor där utveckling och Sveriges säkerhet står i fokus. Vi växer kontinuerligt för att på ett effektivt sätt kunna ta om hand vårt viktiga uppdrag. Välkommen till oss.

Om anställning på ISP Anställningen är tillsvidare och inleds med sex månaders provanställning. Tillträde sker enligt överenskommelse. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras.

För att arbeta hos oss krävs svenskt medborgarskap. Befattningen är placerad i säkerhetsklass och en säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll kommer att genomföras innan beslut om anställning fattas. Då en stor del av arbetsuppgifterna omfattar sekretessbelagd information är möjligheten till distansarbete begränsat.

Om ISP ISP är en statlig myndighet med utrikespolitisk inriktning. Våra uppdrag är exportkontroll av strategiska produkter samt granskning av utländska investeringar. Vi är också nationell myndighet för kemvapenkonventionen och hanterar internationella sanktioner. Läs mer om oss på isp.se. ISP är en växande specialistmyndighet belägen i Solna/Stockholm. Vi rekryterar löpande till nya uppdrag och hos oss arbetar statsvetare, jurister, ingenjörer och många andra experter. Vi är drygt hundra medarbetare som tillsammans skapar en varm och välkomnande atmosfär. Vi menar att våra olikheter är en styrka då arbetet kräver flexibelt tänkande och att kunna ta in olika perspektiv. Hos oss får du arbeta i rymliga och fräscha lokaler som ger arbetsro för komplexa frågeställningar. Vårt arbete sker huvudsakligen på kontoret då säkerhet är i fokus för oss. Utöver goda utvecklingsmöjligheter erbjuder vi flexibel arbetstid, friskvårdstimme och friskvårdsbidrag på 5 000 kr per år. Semestern är 28 - 35 dagar per år (beroende på din ålder) och du har möjlighet till löneväxling. De statliga kollektivavtalen ger dig trygg anställning och bra tjänstepension. Läs mer om våra förmåner på isp.se. Vi arbetar utifrån den statliga värdegrunden och vårt fokus ligger på legalitet och objektivitet samt service och effektivitet. Det är viktigt för oss att du delar dessa värderingar.

Ansökan Välkommen med din ansökan i form av CV, examensbevis, samt personligt brev senast den 15 september 2025. Ange referensnummer 2025-9.2-0049. Enligt Riksarkivets föreskrifter gallras ansökningshandlingar från ej antagna sökande två år efter att tillsättningsbeslutet vunnit laga kraft. Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt försäljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

companyDescription:
Välkommen till oss på Länsförsäkringar! Länsförsäkringsgruppen består av 23 lokala och kundägda länsförsäkringsbolag runt om i hela Sverige som tillsammans äger utvecklings- och servicebolaget Länsförsäkringar AB i Stockholm. 

Vi erbjuder våra kunder helhetslösningar inom bank, försäkring och fastighetsförmedling. Genom våra dotterbolag Agria och LF Finans erbjuder vi djurförsäkring respektive finansieringslösningar. En stor del av Länsförsäkringars framgångar ligger i balansen mellan lokalt och gemensamt. 

Vill du vara en del av något större? Vi har lediga jobb inom flera olika kategorier, du hittar dem under fliken "Visa jobb". Hos oss är du med och skapar trygghet för både människor och samhällen. Att vara en del av något större blir en naturlig del av din vardag.
companySlogan: 23 lokala och kundägda länsförsäkringsbolag som tillsammans äger service- och utvecklingsbolaget Länsförsäkringar AB.
descriptionHtml:
Vill du vara med och driva hållbarhetsarbetet i en av Sveriges mest kundnära finansiella organisationer? Hos oss får du möjlighet att påverka hur hållbarhet integreras i investeringsbeslut och bidra till långsiktigt värdeskapande. Vi söker nu en engagerad hållbarhetsanalytiker till vårt dedikerade team.


Om jobbet

Som hållbarhetsanalytiker hos oss får du en central roll i att utveckla och stärka hållbarhetsarbetet kopplat till våra globala aktiefonder. Du blir en del av ett engagerat hållbarhetsteam som arbetar nära både förvaltare och andra funktioner inom fondförvaltningen. Här får du möjlighet att bidra till att integrera hållbarhet i investeringsprocessen och vara med och påverka hur vi skapar värde för våra kunder på ett ansvarsfullt sätt.


Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:

  • Bedöma hållbarhetsrisker och möjligheter i globala bolag
  • Bidra med input till förvaltarna för de globala aktiefonderna
  • Redogöra för vår hållbarhetssyn på bolag i de globala fonderna vid frågor, granskningar, rapportering och presentationer
  • Utveckla integreringen av hållbarhet i investeringsprocessen, exempelvis genom användning av hållbarhetsdata och relevanta indikatorer
  • Samarbeta med hållbarhetsteamet och övriga förvaltare, samt bidra till påverkansarbete och rapportering


Vem är du?

Följande kommer göra dig framgångsrik i denna tjänst:

  • Eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi, finans eller annat relevant område
  • Minst 3 års erfarenhet av aktieanalys, gärna med fokus på hållbarhet och ESG
  • Stark analytisk förmåga och erfarenhet av att använda finansiella analysverktyg
  • God förståelse för hållbarhetsprinciper och hur dessa påverkar företagsvärdering
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift
  • Utmärkta kommunikations- och presentationsfärdigheter


Som person trivs du med att arbeta både självständigt och i team. Du har ett proaktivt och lösningsorienterat förhållningssätt och motiveras av att hitta nya vägar för att driva hållbarhetsarbetet framåt. Du är analytisk, noggrann och har lätt för att samarbeta med olika funktioner och personer.


Om oss

Länsförsäkringar Fondförvaltning är en del av Länsförsäkringsgruppen, en av Sveriges mest respekterade och kundnära finansiella aktörer. Vi erbjuder ett brett utbud av fonder och investeringstjänster med fokus på hållbarhet, kvalitet och långsiktighet. Hos oss får du arbeta i en dynamisk och framåtblickande organisation där innovation, ansvarstagande och kundens behov alltid står i centrum.


Mer information

Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.

Sista ansökningsdag: 2025-09-12

companyDescription:
Universal Investment is a pioneer in the investment industry and has been successful in the market since 1968. Written in our DNA is our ability to offer funds services from a single source, as an independent and visionary organisation. We provide a reliable outlook for investors and asset managers.

What makes us tick? Universal Investment stands for creating convincing and innovative business models. Consequently, we help asset managers worldwide, concentrate on their core expertise – implementing their investment strategies successfully – while we take care of everything else. We enable institutional investors to bundle their assets in all asset classes
through our ManCo platform and administer their assets efficiently. Our clients are on the safe side of current and future regulations with us. We are a true partner and are independent without any conflicts of interest. 

We are already the market leader for white label funds. We are also the largest third-party ManCo as well as the largest AIFM in Luxembourg. Furthermore, we are one of the few providers active in the three most important European fund domiciles: Ireland, Luxembourg and Germany. We administer more than 1 trillion EUR in assets.

The Universal Investment Group continues to move forward on its way to becoming the leading European fund service platform and Super ManCo for all asset classes. We are working on this with about 1,700 employees in Frankfurt, Luxembourg, Dublin, London, Paris, Stockholm and Krakow.

We are continuing to expand and are actively looking for new colleagues. Simply get in touch with us and become part of our success story!

Imprint: https://www.universal-investment.com/en/Imprint/
Data Privacy: https://www.universal-investment.com/en/Data-Privacy/
companySlogan: Improving business together
descriptionHtml:
Grow with us!

We are currently looking for a colleague to join our AML / KYC Team that is a part of our Customer Due Diligence Department.


Your tasks and responsibilities:


  • Conduct initial due diligence for new investors, collecting AML/KYC documentation based on risk level and investor type
  • Perform periodic reviews of existing investors and assessments when circumstances change
  • Update investor information in systems, upload documents, and complete AML/CTF risk assessments
  • Manage investor and client inquiries via email and phone
  • Collaborate with internal teams to resolve investor-related queries


Your skills and experience:


  • Previous experience in AML / KYC
  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, Economics, or related field
  • Fluent in Swedish and English (Norwegian proficiency is an advantage)
  • Strong analytical mindset with problem-solving orientation
  • Excellent written communication and summarization skills
  • Proactive approach with strong attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines
  • Self-motivated with initiative to work effectively across diverse functions



What we offer:


  • An exciting working environment and challenging tasks
  • Extensive insight into the European fund industry
  • Friendly, international and goal-oriented working environment
  • High degree of independent work
  • Pension Scheme
  • Employee group life insurance


About us:

descriptionHtml:
IK Partners (“IK”) is a European private equity firm focused on investments in the Benelux, DACH, France, Nordics and the UK. Since 1989, IK has raised more than €19 billion of capital and invested in over 200 European companies. IK supports companies with strong underlying potential, partnering with management teams and investors to create robust, well-positioned businesses with excellent long-term prospects. For more information, visit www.ikpartners.com.

We are looking for interns to join IK’s Nordic deal teams. The internship is envisaged to last for 5-6 months on a full-time basis from January/February 2026.

The Private Equity Internship would be well suited for a student or recent graduate who would like to broaden their experience in the Private Equity industry. The role is both broad and varied as you support all strategies in the office: Development Capital, Small Cap, Mid Cap and Partnership Fund. You will support our Associates and Analysts whilst reporting to the Partners. We are looking for strong academics, excellent data analysis skills and it is very important that the candidate is both confident and has an eye for detail.

Please consider that this role requires a full-time commitment. The application process will run until 30 September 2025 and we evaluate applications on a rolling basis.

The Role

You will work closely with the investment professionals of IK's Nordics Deal Teams and will assist with the appraisal of potential investment opportunities (both quantitative and qualitative) in the Nordics region. The work of an intern is highly diverse, and candidates will gain investing, financial and operational experience across a wide range of sectors. Tasks include:

  • Deal sourcing; sourcing exercises and updating pipeline information
  • Analyse potential investment opportunities
  • Carry out research and prepare background information
  • Business and operational analysis
  • Assist with internal presentations
  • Assist the team with portfolio reporting and project related work
  • Financial modelling and valuation


Our Expectations

  • Relevant academic focus, e.g. finance/economics and engineering
  • Excellent academic track record
  • Prior first work experience through relevant internships in private equity/ venture capital, investment banking/ M&A advisory, strategic consulting or comparable internships.
  • Financially literate and analytical mindset
  • High level of attention to detail
  • Fluency in Swedish and English is a requirement


Our Offering

  • Dynamic and supportive working environment with on-the-job training provided
  • Insight into the workings of a leading European private equity firm
  • Opportunity to work with a broad range of colleagues in a diverse and inclusive workplace culture

descriptionHtml:
Deloitte is a global leader in Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal and Consultative Services. With more than 450,000 employees in over 150 countries, we help our clients and our communities solve complex problems and achieve ambitious goals through domain and industry expertise, global reach and alliances with the largest tech providers. What defines Deloitte is our drive to make a meaningful difference. For us, this means long-term development and results for our clients and their companies, ideas and actions that also contribute to positive societal development. It is also about empowering our people and promoting their development.


Job Description

In M&A Transaction Services we support our clients in connection to acquisition or sale of companies by providing insightful financial analysis. Our work involves reviewing historical trading and balance sheets, assessing forecast consistency, and evaluating operational and financial assets and liabilities to help clients make well-informed decisions. Our clients include both domestic and international private equity firms and strategic corporates.


As an Analyst in our M&A Transaction Services team, you will contribute by analysing financial data, preparing agendas for management interviews, and drafting due diligence reports. We highly value teamwork and collaboration, and you will work closely with experienced colleagues who will support your growth and development. Throughout your work, you are expected to demonstrate professionalism, integrity and a strong work ethic. This role offers a unique opportunity to build expertise in managing due diligence processes for both buyers and sellers of companies.


We welcome applications from candidates with a master’s degree who are eager to join our team in Stockholm or Malmö in August 2026.


Qualifications

We are looking for individuals who:


  • Have a graduate degree (MBA/Master’s or equivalent) with strong academic results


  • Are interested in financial analysis and motivated to understand business dynamics


  • Enjoy working collaboratively and are motivated to learn and grow in a supportive environment


  • Communicate effectively in English and preferably a Scandinavian language


  • Are proficient in MS Excel and PowerPoint


  • Approach challenges with a positive and flexible mindset, managing deadlines constructively




Additional Information

What we offer


As a part of Deloitte you get access to the world’s largest consultancy network, meaning you have colleagues all over the globe who you can utilize in order to develop yourself, our clients and our business. You will work with exciting and complex projects both on a global and Nordic level within a team who values your perspective. Your personal development and the opportunities that help you grow is of great importance to us. We offer you practical experience, a personal coach as well as continuous feedback and input from more experienced colleagues.


You get the opportunity to be part of an organization where we help our clients on their journey to become a more sustainable organization. You also get to be involved in our own initiatives regarding climate and environmental work as well as social responsibility.


How to apply


Submit your application online as soon as possible. Your application should contain your:


  • CV


  • University grades. The grade document you upload should have been retrieved no more than 2 months ago




In the first step, you upload your CV. In the next step, you upload your university transcripts (for courses completed so far) and answer questions related to the role, including a motivation for why you have applied for this role. Please note that you need to submit all the requested documents for your application to be considered completed. Apply as soon as possible as we review applications on an ongoing basis. Upon submitting your application, you should receive a confirmation email from us. If you don't receive this email, we advise you to submit your application again. If you are applying for multiple positions with us, please clarify in your applications which role you are most interested in.


Questions


If you have any questions regarding the application process, please contact Sophie Karlerö Kolijn, Recruiter, at skarlero@deloitte.se.


As a qualitative part of Deloitte's recruitment process, our final candidates undergo a background check, in order to increase awareness of our future employees, secure the working environment for our employees and meet regulatory requirements. The process for background checks is carried out in collaboration with ToFindOut. https://tofindout.se/en/background-check/. For the roles where university grades are requested a verification of the grades is made as a part of the recruitment process. If you have any questions, please contact the responsible recruiter.


What impact will you make?


Every day, your work will make an impact that matters, while you thrive in a dynamic culture of inclusion, collaboration and high performance. We do not just aspire to make solutions. We wish to create an impact that makes a positive and crucial difference for the people around us, our clients and our society. Deloitte is where you’ll find unlimited opportunities to succeed and realize your full potential.

companyDescription:
If is the largest insurance company in the Nordics, with over 4.5 million customers, both individuals and businesses. We insure one in five homes and passenger cars in the Nordics and offer personal insurance for individuals and businesses. 

We have 10,000 employees, spread across several business areas and countries, who together ensure that our private and business customers receive the right help. 
Thanks to our size and breadth, we can be close to our customers and offer reliable insurances. Our promise to our customers is the core of our identity – If helps a lot. 

If is owned by the Finnish company Sampo, which is listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange in Helsinki, Stockholm, and Copenhagen. In 2025, Topdanmark became part of If. If has its headquarters in Sweden and also operates in the Baltics. 

Would you like to work with us? We encourage qualified candidates to apply, regardless of age, gender, disability, cultural or ethnic background.

If Skadeforsäkring - Sweden https://www.if.se
If Skadeforsiking - Norway https://www.if.no/
If Skadeforsikring - Danmark https://www.if.dk/
If Vakuutus - Finland https://www.if.fi/
descriptionHtml:
Will you take Solvency II reporting to the next level?


Our mission is to ensure regulatory compliance and to build trust with the Financial Supervisory Authorities through accurate and reliable reporting. As a Risk Reporting Analyst at If, you will gain a broad perspective on the insurance business and the regulatory landscape within the leading P&C insurance company in the Nordic region.


If you are passionate about analyzing and reporting insurance data, and enjoy turning complex issues into clear and actionable insights, then this could be the perfect opportunity for you.


Is this your new challenge?

As a Risk Reporting Analyst, you will play a key role in our team, contributing to both internal and external Solvency II reporting with a strong focus on quantitative analysis. You will be responsible for preparing and analyzing reports – primarily of a quantitative nature – for both management and regulatory authorities. A central part of your role will be managing asset and financial reporting templates, where you will also act as the team’s go-to expert for technical questions and process improvements. By working with both quantitative reporting and, to some extent, qualitative reporting, you will gain valuable insights into If’s capital management framework and how we help create long-term security for our policyholders. This position offers excellent opportunities for professional growth. Based on your interests and ambitions, you will have the chance to broaden your skills and knowledge across a wide range of areas within the team. You will collaborate closely with many parts of the organization, including the CFO organization, Risk Management, IT, Investments, and Insurance Operations.


As a Risk Reporting Analyst, you will join our Solvency & Capital Reporting team consisting five people. This team is part of Corporate Reporting & Cash Management unit of 20 people with the majority working at the Bergshamra office in Sweden. Corporate Reporting is, in addition to the responsibilities of Solvency & Capital Reporting team, also responsible for the group accounting principles, consolidation, annual reporting, narrative Solvency II reporting as well as If’s framework for internal control.


Your Profile

To thrive in this role, you are someone who takes ownership of your work, sees tasks through to completion, and proactively identifies opportunities for improvement. You value teamwork and understand the importance of collaborating to achieve shared goals while supporting and lifting your colleagues. You bring a high level of accuracy and quality to your work, combined with a curious mindset and a strong desire to continuously learn and grow. As the position involves many points of contact across the organization, you are attentive and responsive to others, and you communicate clearly and effectively.


You hold a relevant academic degree from a university or college and have acquired several years of relevant work experience. Ideally, you are currently working in risk, audit, or accounting/reporting, with a connection to the banking, finance, or insurance industry. You have strong analytical skills and the ability to interpret and manage complex data. Experience with Excel, SQL, or Power Query is highly valued. You are fluent in English and have proficiency in a Nordic language, both written and spoken. Previous experience with Solvency II or Finrep/Corep is considered an advantage.


What If offer

In the same way that we place high demands on you as an employee, we also expect you to place high demands on us as an employer. Here are some of the benefits of working at If:

  • An including work environment where everyone is welcome
  • Career and development opportunities in the biggest insurance company in the Nordics
  • Social activities, as well as highly skilled professional environment
  • Possibility of hybrid workplace
  • Health promoting workplace with e.g., wellness allowance and various sports activities
  • Great insurance benefits


Application

In this recruitment, If is collaborating with Prodiem. Selection and interviews will be conducted on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible. Applications are made via Prodiem’s website www.prodiem.se . For questions, please contact Jakob Altensten at Prodiem, phone +46 73 344 51 80.


Welcome with your application!

descriptionHtml:
Deloitte är en globalt ledande leverantör av Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal och Consultative Services. Med mer än 450 000 medarbetare i över 150 länder hjälper vi våra klienter och vår omvärld att lösa komplexa problem och nå ambitiösa mål genom områdes- och branschexpertis, global räckvidd och allianser med de största tech-leverantörerna. Det som definierar Deloitte är vår strävan efter att göra en meningsfull skillnad. För oss innebär det långsiktig utveckling och resultat för våra klienter och deras företag, idéer och handlingar som också bidrar till en positiv samhällsutveckling. Det handlar även om att stärka våra medarbetare och främja deras utveckling.


Jobbeskrivning

Finance Transformation inom Deloitte sysselsätter cirka 250 medarbetare i Sverige, fördelat på våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö. Vi arbetar med att supportera ledande företag och organisationer inom alla typer av branscher med att driva transformationer i projektform och som tillfälligt resursstöd.


CFO Services

Inom CFO Services, som är en del av Finance Transformation, arbetar du primärt på plats hos våra klienter i temporära roller som redovisningsekonom, controller eller ekonomichef, men även som resurs- och kompetensstöd vid genomförande av projekt och transformationer. Våra klienter varierar från mindre bolag till internationella börsnoterade koncerner. Vi anställer till våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö. 


Kvalifikationer

Vi söker vi dig som:


  • Är nyutexaminerad ekonom med magister-eller masterexamen.


  • Inriktning mot redovisning, controlling, management eller ekonomi & IT.


  • Relevant erfarenhet av ekonomiarbete, gärna inom redovisning


  • Har goda språkkunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.




Meriterande:


  • Goda akademiska meriter, internationell erfarenhet och relevant engagemang vid sidan av studierna


  • Erfarenhet inom konsultbranschen




Inom Finance Transformation är du en lagspelare med hög samarbetsförmåga och stark analytisk förmåga. Du trivs med att arbeta självständigt, är utåtriktad, målmedveten, engagerad, initiativrik och drivs av nya utmaningar.


Ytterligare information

Vi erbjuder

Som en del av Deloitte får du tillgång till världens största konsultnätverk tillsammans med kunniga och engagerade kollegor som är experter inom sina områden. Du får arbeta med spännande, komplexa uppdrag, både på global och nordisk nivå, i team som alltid värderar ditt perspektiv. Vi lägger stor vikt vid att erbjuda dig möjligheter att driva din personliga utveckling. Vi erbjuder dig praktisk erfarenhet, en personlig coach som långsiktigt stöttar dig i din karriär och kontinuerlig feedback och support från mer erfarna kollegor. 


Du får möjlighet att arbeta i ett företag som hjälper andra aktörer i deras resa mot en mer hållbar organisation, samtidigt som vi arbetar målmedvetet med våra egna ambitioner inom både klimat, miljö och socialt ansvar. Vi ser ett värde i att våra medarbetare har olika erfarenheter vilket främjar nytänkande och bidrar till fler perspektiv.


Ansökan

Du ansöker online. Din ansökan ska bestå av:


  • CV


  • Betygskopior från högskola/universitet - De betyg som du laddar upp bör inte vara utdragna senare än 2 månader tillbaka i tiden.




I det första steget laddar du upp ditt CV. I nästa steg laddar du upp dina universitetsbetyg (över hittills avklarade kurser) samt svarar på frågor som är kopplade till rollen, inklusive en motivering till varför du sökt denna roll. Observera att både CV samt fullständiga betyg behöver laddas upp för att din ansökan ska ses som fullständig. Vi tillämpar löpande urval, så skicka gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Vid inskickad ansökan ska du få ett bekräftelsemejl från oss. Får du inte detta mejl råder vi dig att skicka in din ansökan på nytt. Om du söker flera tjänster hos oss ber vi dig att i dina ansökningar tydliggöra vilken roll du är mest intresserad av.


Som ett kvalitativt led i Deloittes rekryteringsprocess genomgår våra slutkandidater en bakgrundskontroll, detta för att öka kännedomen om våra framtida medarbetare, trygga arbetsmiljön för våra medarbetare och möta regelkrav. Processen för bakgrundskontroller genomförs i samarbete med ToFindOut. https://tofindout.se/sv/faq/. För de tjänster som Deloitte efterfrågar universitetsbetyg genomförs en verifiering som en del i rekryteringsprocessen. För frågor hänvisar vi till ansvarig rekryterare i processen.


Frågor

Om du har frågor om tjänsten eller om rekryteringsprocessen är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Johanna Aspegårdh, jaspegardh@deloitte.se


Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!


What impact will you make?


På Deloitte gör ditt arbete en meningsfull skillnad, varje dag, medan du växer i en inkluderande företagskultur av samarbete och hög prestation. Utöver att skapa lösningar strävar vi efter att göra en positiv och avgörande skillnad för andra i vår omgivning, våra klienter och samhället. På Deloitte hittar du tidigare dolda möjligheter att lyckas och inser din fulla potential.

Med mer än 450 000 medarbetare i över 150 länder hjälper vi våra klienter och vår omvärld att lösa komplexa problem och nå ambitiösa mål genom områdes- och branschexpertis, global räckvidd och allianser med de största tech-leverantörerna. Det som definierar Deloitte är vår strävan efter att göra en meningsfull skillnad. För oss innebär det långsiktig utveckling och resultat för våra klienter och deras företag, idéer och handlingar som också bidrar till en positiv samhällsutveckling. Det handlar även om att stärka våra medarbetare och främja deras utveckling.JobbeskrivningFinance Transformation inom Deloitte sysselsätter cirka 250 medarbetare i Sverige, fördelat på våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö. Vi arbetar med att supportera ledande företag och organisationer inom alla typer av branscher med att driva transformationer i projektform och som tillfälligt resursstöd. CFO Services Inom CFO Services, som är en del av Finance Transformation, arbetar du primärt på plats hos våra klienter i temporära roller som redovisningsekonom, controller eller ekonomichef, men även som resurs- och kompetensstöd vid genomförande av projekt och transformationer.  Våra klienter varierar från mindre bolag till internationella börsnoterade koncerner. Vi anställer till våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö.  KvalifikationerVi söker vi dig som: Är nyutexaminerad ekonom med magister-eller masterexamen.  Inriktning mot redovisning, controlling, management eller ekonomi & IT.  Relevant erfarenhet av ekonomiarbete, gärna inom redovisning Har goda språkkunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.Meriterande: Goda akademiska meriter, internationell erfarenhet och relevant engagemang vid sidan av studierna Erfarenhet inom konsultbranschen Inom Finance Transformation är du en lagspelare med hög samarbetsförmåga och stark analytisk förmåga. Du trivs med att arbeta självständigt, är utåtriktad, målmedveten, engagerad, initiativrik och drivs av nya utmaningar.  Ytterligare informationVi erbjuder Som en del av Deloitte får du tillgång till världens största konsultnätverk tillsammans med kunniga och engagerade kollegor som är experter inom sina områden. Du får arbeta med spännande, komplexa uppdrag, både på global och nordisk nivå, i team som alltid värderar ditt perspektiv. Vi lägger stor vikt vid att erbjuda dig möjligheter att driva din personliga utveckling. Vi erbjuder dig praktisk erfarenhet, en personlig coach som långsiktigt stöttar dig i din karriär och kontinuerlig feedback och support från mer erfarna kollegor.  Du får möjlighet att arbeta i ett företag som hjälper andra aktörer i deras resa mot en mer hållbar organisation, samtidigt som vi arbetar målmedvetet med våra egna ambitioner inom både klimat, miljö och socialt ansvar. Vi ser ett värde i att våra medarbetare har olika erfarenheter vilket främjar nytänkande och bidrar till fler perspektiv. Ansökan Du ansöker online. Din ansökan ska bestå av:  CV  Betygskopior från högskola/universitet - De betyg som du laddar upp bör inte vara utdragna senare än 2 månader tillbaka i tiden. I det första steget laddar du upp ditt CV. I nästa steg laddar du upp dina universitetsbetyg (över hittills avklarade kurser) samt svarar på frågor som är kopplade till rollen, inklusive en motivering till varför du sökt denna roll. Observera att både CV samt fullständiga betyg behöver laddas upp för att din ansökan ska ses som fullständig. Vi tillämpar löpande urval, så skicka gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Vid inskickad ansökan ska du få ett bekräftelsemejl från oss. Får du inte detta mejl råder vi dig att skicka in din ansökan på nytt. Om du söker flera tjänster hos oss ber vi dig att i dina ansökningar tydliggöra vilken roll du är mest intresserad av. Som ett kvalitativt led i Deloittes rekryteringsprocess genomgår våra slutkandidater en bakgrundskontroll, detta för att öka kännedomen om våra framtida medarbetare, trygga arbetsmiljön för våra medarbetare och möta regelkrav. Processen för bakgrundskontroller genomförs i samarbete med ToFindOut. https://tofindout.se/sv/faq/. För de tjänster som Deloitte efterfrågar universitetsbetyg genomförs en verifiering som en del i rekryteringsprocessen. För frågor hänvisar vi till ansvarig rekryterare i processen. Frågor  Om du har frågor om tjänsten eller om rekryteringsprocessen är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Johanna Aspegårdh, jaspegardh@deloitte.se  Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! What impact will you make?På Deloitte gör ditt arbete en meningsfull skillnad, varje dag, medan du växer i en inkluderande företagskultur av samarbete och hög prestation. Utöver att skapa lösningar strävar vi efter att göra en positiv och avgörande skillnad för andra i vår omgivning, våra klienter och samhället. På Deloitte hittar du tidigare dolda möjligheter att lyckas och inser din fulla potential.

companyDescription:
We are a universal bank with a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies – enabling dreams and aspirations for a greater good. Every day, we work to support our customers’ financial development, delivering best-in-class omnichannel customer experiences and driving sustainable change. 

We are a full-service universal bank and the third largest corporation in the Nordic region and one of the top 10 financial services companies in Europe based on market capitalisation. We are present in 17 countries, including our four Nordic home markets which together constitute the 10th largest economy in the world. 

As demand for digital services increases, we're continuously working to become your future digital bank by improving the products and services we offer, such as new online banking platforms, mobile payments and analytics tools, to name a few.

The Nordea share is listed on the Nasdaq Helsinki, Nasdaq Copenhagen and Nasdaq Stockholm exchanges. Read more about us at nordea.com.

We value your opinions and welcome your comments and questions on our posts here on LinkedIn. Please note that we reply mainly during business hours, Monday-Friday. Please keep a polite, professional and constructive tone. We remove comments containing crude language and derogatory views of our staff and other people who comment on our posts. We do not allow content that is unrelated to the subject, and we remove discriminatory and racist comments as well as spam and advertising.
companySlogan: Nordea is the largest financial services group in the Nordic region and one of the biggest banks in Europe.
descriptionHtml:
Job ID: 30720

Are you looking for a career within Banking and Sales? Would learning to identify and advice in regards to debt related financial risks and translate it into clear solutions to Corporate clients be the type of challenge you are looking for? Then you have come to the right place!

We are now looking for an Analyst to join our Derivatives Coverage team at Nordea Markets. The profile we’re looking for will service our customers with advice and sparring on interest rate risks and drive initiatives together with internal stakeholders. You`ll join a team with a mix of brilliant minds that hope to find an additional colleague whom can complement our current team members’ different personalities, background and experiences.

At Nordea, we see that the world is changing fast – and we want to be one step ahead of the curve. That is why we’re deeply committed to providing the financial solutions of tomorrow to our customers. We are creating an agile environment where we experiment and grow together – and we need your ideas and unique background. With us, you will be in great company with a chance to make your mark on something bigger.

About This Opportunity

Welcome to the Derivatives Coverage Sales team. We are part of the FX/Derivatives Sales and Research organisation within Nordea Markets. We offer corporate customers advice on how to manage currency & interest rate related risks. The advice is based on in-depth customer understanding, market research, product knowledge, laws and regulations in order to build long lasting relations.

In the Derivatives team you will play a valuable role in nurturing our close relationships with corporate customers and stakeholders. You will get a motivating and unique opportunity to build a robust capital markets and financial profile.

In this role, you will therefore quickly be given the responsibility to work with clients and across the full spectrum of our derivatives solutions. Eventually you will grow into your role as the “derivatives know how person” clients and other colleagues in the bank go to for advice. As a unit, we value great team work as much as you can work independently, being proactive and have a creative mindset to contribute with new ideas to serve our clients at our best!

What you’ll be doing:

  • Support colleagues with sales pitches/presentations, pricing and trade ideas towards our customers
  • Develop yours skills in advising our corporate clients on interest rate hedging (i.e. swaps, caps, loan-products) and acquisition of new clients
  • Generate ideas, conduct interest rate risk analysis and supporting the structuring of customer oriented solutions
  • Pricing and execution of interest rate hedging transactions with customers
  • Collaboration with other Internal Units and stake holders within the customer team

You`ll join the Swedish Derivatives Coverage Sales team where ownership and collaboration are the corner stones in what we do in order to consistently excel clients expectations. The role is based in Stockholm.

Who You Are

Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the four key values that guide us in being at our best. We believe you enjoy both collaborative and independent work, are curious to learn and that you see yourself as a team player. You’re reliable, willing to speak up – and committed to empowering others, the same way as you will be empowered by your colleagues.

To succeed in this role, we believe that you are/have:

  • A passion for people, serving clients and building relationships
  • A team oriented professional with a sincere interest in the financial markets
  • Proactive, goal-oriented and highly motivated to take responsibility and grow
  • Driven and willing to work hard in order to climb a steep learning curve, exceed expectations and close deals
  • Professional spoken and written communication skills, both in English and Swedish

We welcome all applicants who has a great interest in the financial markets and holds at least a University degree. No prior experience within the derivatives field is required but earlier experience from Sales and Banking or similar is a plus.

More information

At nordea.com, you can read more about us, our business and our focus on integrating sustainability into everything we do. To get more insight in the daily life at Nordea, please visit our blog: https://www.nordea.com/en/careers/meet-our-talents

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 11/09/2025. For more information about the position please contact Head of Derivatives Sales, Axel Arenlind at axel.arenlind@nordea.com.

At Nordea, we know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable workplace. We deeply believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us better at serving customers and communities. So please come as you are.

Please be aware that any applications or CVs coming through email or direct messages will not be accepted or considered.

If you are an external consultant interested in this role and wish to apply please contact the hiring people leader for separate instruction on how to apply.

companyDescription:
Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions.

The company was founded in 2017 by Mikael Hussain, Sven Perkmann, and Filip Polhem, all of whom have extensive experience from tech giants like Klarna, Spotify, and iZettle. Anyfin is currently operating in its home market of Sweden as well as in Germany, Finland, and Norway.

Backed by investors Accel, EQT Ventures, Northzone, Fintech Collective, Global Founders Capital, Quadrille Capital, Augmentum FinTech, and Citi Ventures, amongst others.
companySlogan: Fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions.
descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

At Anyfin, we believe finance should work in favor of the consumer – not the other way around. That’s why we build smart, transparent and customer-first solutions that make everyday finances easier and fairer.

Now we’re looking for a Senior Credit Analyst to help us take the next step. This is a key role for someone who thrives on analytical challenges and enjoys working hands-on in a fast-moving environment where your voice really matters.

Join us to shape how credit should be done – better.

What You Will Be Doing

Own and develop our credit strategies to strike the right balance between growth, risk and profitability.

Monitor and analyze credit portfolio performance across markets and products.

Define and refine our underwriting criteria – from new product launches to continuous improvement.

Collaborate closely with Analytics, Product, Finance and Operations to align credit with company goals.

Bring clarity and insight to credit decisions, policies, and edge cases.

Develop pricing strategies, customer segmentation and forecasting models.

Present your recommendations to senior stakeholders – and have a real impact on how we scale.

About You

You have a few years of experience in credit risk, analytics or a similar field, and you're ready to take a bigger step. You're confident working with data, fluent in SQL, and comfortable navigating large datasets and credit KPIs. You probably have a degree in a quantitative field like Engineering, Mathematics or Economics. But more importantly, you have an analytical mindset, a strong sense of ownership, and the ability to connect credit insights to broader business decisions.

You're curious, collaborative and excited to join a team where things move fast and your ideas can help shape the way forward. And if you're a bit earlier in your journey but bring the right skills and mindset – we’re still interested.

What We Offer

At Anyfin, we’re building more than just credit products – we’re building a company grounded in trust, transparency and a whole lot of heart. With a flat structure, a high pace and zero prestige, you’ll be working closely with leadership and have a real say in the big decisions. You’ll join a diverse team of kind, ambitious people who genuinely care about each other, about our customers, and about doing things better.

We know that great people come from different paths. Whether you’ve led credit strategy before or are ready to take a big step earlier in your career, we want to hear from you.

We’re backed by some of the best investors in Europe, and we’ve already built products that make a meaningful difference in people’s lives. But we’re only just getting started. If you're looking for a place where you’ll be challenged, supported, and inspired by the people around you, this might be the one.

We can’t wait to meet you.

About Anyfin

Who are we?

Anyfin is an innovative startup with a mission to help people to become their best financial self. We know that money can be tricky for many, therefore we wanted to create an easier way of understanding and improving your finances.

What do we do?

Anyfin is a smart financial-app where you get a great overview of your spendings, income, subscriptions and much more. We also provide a service that helps lowering the cost of consumer loans and credits by reducing high interests, and removing unnecessary fees. Overall, the app is a great tool to understand your finances better.

What about the future?

We are constantly growing and by 2024 we will launch even more services. Fun, huh? Come join our journey and work with ambitious people from all over the world. (Our office is based in Stockholm.)

Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions. We help consumers reduce the costs of high-interest loans, and take control of their repayment plan.

companyDescription:
MCF Corporate Finance (MCF) is a leading M&A and debt advisory firm with offices in Frankfurt, Hamburg, Helsinki, London, Munich and Stockholm. Under the brand D.A. Davidson MCF International, we identify and execute transatlantic M&A transactions.

MCF has successfully structured and executed over 1,200 M&A and debt advisory transactions for multinational corporations, mid-sized companies, family offices, and private equity investors across its four core sectors: Business Services, Consumer, Industrials and Technology.
companySlogan: Our mission is to provide honest and relationship-driven M&A & debt advice, tailored to each client's business journey.
descriptionHtml:
Join our growing team

Join our Stockholm team for an M&A internship during approx. 20 weeks starting in January 2026.

What You'll Do

  • Gain experience and support the team in all aspects of M&A transactions
  • Strong involvement in sell- and buy-side projects and current mandates
  • Work on pitch preparations, target and investor identifications, as well as management presentations
  • Conduct market, industry and company research
  • Work on financial modelling and valuation analyses

About You

  • Top academic performance in Economics, Finance or a related discipline with at least 4 semesters completed
  • Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills
  • Fluent in Swedish and English
  • Energy, enthusiasm and commitment to work in a highly focused team environment
  • Exceptional analytical and communication skills
  • Previous experience in corporate finance, appreciated but not required

What We Offer

  • Broad experience with M&A transactions in an international environment
  • Exciting range of tasks and valuable on-the-job training
  • Strong team-spirit and support from all team-members
  • Flat-hierarchies with an opportunity to learn from experienced professionals

Apply by submitting your CV and cover letter no later than September 28th.

Contact information

Jana Annies

+49 15756613197

annies@mcfcorpfin.com

About Us

MCF is a leading independent M&A and Debt Advisory firm with a focus on cross-border and mid-market transactions. Our multinational team works from 6 offices across Northern Europe and with the support of our transatlantic partner D.A. Davidson & Co., we provide best-in-class deal capabilities for large corporates, leading mid-sized companies, private equity and family offices worldwide.

To find out more about us, our benefits, culture and commitment to diversity and inclusion, visit our website. We strongly encourage candidates from all backgrounds and especially underrepresented groups to apply – we value different points of view.

companyDescription:
NMG Consulting is a leading multinational consulting firm focusing solely on investments, insurance and reinsurance markets. NMG’s vertically-integrated model - consulting backed by ‘information advantage’ from proprietary research - differentiates us from the competition. We work with financial institutions (insurers, reinsurers, fund managers and pension funds, banks and brokers) to shape strategy, implement change and manage performance.
 
NMG Insights reports tap into the information flow from our consulting case experience, research and analytics programmes globally. We deliberately select topic areas that we believe do not attract sufficient attention, or where our views run contrary to the accepted wisdom. Insights reports are necessarily high level, and we do not publish client-specific data or detail from our proprietary studies. Sample NMG Insights reports can be found on the following page nmg-consulting.com/publications/reports/.
 
NMG Consulting is a part of the NMG Group, an Independent, well established global financial services firm with approximately 800 employees across Asia Pacific, Europe, North America and South Africa.
companySlogan: Insurance. Reinsurance. Wealth/Retirement. Asset Management.
descriptionHtml:
SUMMARY STATEMENT

The Study

NMG conducts an annual Global Asset Management Study (now in its 11th cycle), across 19 markets in North America, EMEA, and Asia Pacific. The study focuses on investment preferences, placement factors, and the views of asset managers. As an example, please click here to view a previous year’s interactive report which highlights asset allocation patterns and global and regional manager rankings for institutional and retail investors.


In 2022, we expanded the study to the Nordics (Denmark, Finland, Norway, Sweden, and Iceland), conducting in-depth interviews with institutional asset owners, asset consultants and retail fund buyers.


The Opportunity

We now have a part-time opportunity for an Independent Consultant to work with NMG on our Global Asset Management Study in Sweden. This program requires executive-style interviews to be undertaken with key decision makers who are involved in investment strategy and asset manager selection. The interview cycle is during August to December and potentially on an annual basis thereafter. This position will suit a highly experienced and self-motivated individual who possesses a strong understanding of asset management in Sweden. This is a rare opportunity to be involved in a progressive consulting engagement in asset management on a flexible working basis.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Success in this role will see the following achieved:

  • Conduct 10 or more interviews with decision makers who influence investment decisions, including fund manager liaison and selection, within the Sweden institutional asset management industry (state and private pension funds, endowments, insurance companies, and asset consultants)
  • Utilise your network to identify executives appropriate to participate in the study
  • Support NMG’s strategy and insights consulting practice in understanding the key themes and insights from the research study and sharing findings back with participants


YOUR EXPERIENCE

The successful candidate for this role will most likely be retired or semi-retired but still seeking to remain in contact with the asset management industry.


They would ideally demonstrate these abilities:

  • 10+ years of experience in the asset management industry with an existing network within parts of the Sweden institutional asset management industry
  • Ability to form relationships and gauge the thinking of key decision makers
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Ability to work effectively with a global team of consultants
  • Excellent attention to detail and high-quality standards
  • Fluency in the local language and English


It is envisioned that this role will be performed on a contract basis, over several days per week for a period of 3 to 4 months, with the potential to contribute on an annual basis.


ABOUT NMG

The NMG Group is a global advisory and intermediary firm focused exclusively on the financial services sector. Established in 1992, NMG has enjoyed rapid expansion internationally and now has over 800 employees in 18 cities worldwide. Our guiding principle is to always ‘Find a Better Way’.


At NMG Consulting we focus exclusively on the Insurance, Reinsurance, Wealth Management and Asset Management Industries. Our advisory model uniquely integrates Consulting, Insights and Analytics. Located globally, we are a diverse team of talented and experienced individuals, partnering with industry leaders to provide deep and powerful insights and knowledge.


We are constantly evolving and the range of work that we do rapidly challenges and expands our peoples’ knowledge. Our team-based structures encourage participation from all experience levels – everyone is encouraged to contribute, regardless of their role. We advocate hearing different points of view, collaboration and constructive debate.


NMG’s Global Insights team specialise in delivering proprietary studies that provide intellectual property to help clients better understand the external market, drivers of success and their competitive positioning. Many of these studies are based on structured executive style interviews with key decision makers within institutional and retail segments.


Our clients are primarily large and/or global asset managers, insurers, and reinsurers. Our combined capabilities deliver an unparalleled depth of insight to support strategic decision-making capabilities at all levels.
descriptionText: SUMMARY STATEMENTThe StudyNMG conducts an annual Global Asset Management Study (now in its 11th cycle), across 19 markets in North America, EMEA, and Asia Pacific. The study focuses on investment preferences, placement factors, and the views of asset managers. As an example, please click here to view a previous year’s interactive report which highlights asset allocation patterns and global and regional manager rankings for institutional and retail investors.In 2022, we expanded the study to the Nordics (Denmark, Finland, Norway, Sweden, and Iceland), conducting in-depth interviews with institutional asset owners, asset consultants and retail fund buyers.The OpportunityWe now have a part-time opportunity for an Independent Consultant to work with NMG on our Global Asset Management Study in Sweden. This program requires executive-style interviews to be undertaken with key decision makers who are involved in investment strategy and asset manager selection. The interview cycle is during August to December and potentially on an annual basis thereafter. This position will suit a highly experienced and self-motivated individual who possesses a strong understanding of asset management in Sweden. This is a rare opportunity to be involved in a progressive consulting engagement in asset management on a flexible working basis.KEY RESPONSIBILITIESSuccess in this role will see the following achieved:Conduct 10 or more interviews with decision makers who influence investment decisions, including fund manager liaison and selection, within the Sweden institutional asset management industry (state and private pension funds, endowments, insurance companies, and asset consultants)Utilise your network to identify executives appropriate to participate in the studySupport NMG’s strategy and insights consulting practice in understanding the key themes and insights from the research study and sharing findings back with participantsYOUR EXPERIENCEThe successful candidate for this role will most likely be retired or semi-retired but still seeking to remain in contact with the asset management industry.They would ideally demonstrate these abilities:10+ years of experience in the asset management industry with an existing network within parts of the Sweden institutional asset management industryAbility to form relationships and gauge the thinking of key decision makersStrong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbalAbility to work effectively with a global team of consultantsExcellent attention to detail and high-quality standardsFluency in the local language and EnglishIt is envisioned that this role will be performed on a contract basis, over several days per week for a period of 3 to 4 months, with the potential to contribute on an annual basis.ABOUT NMGThe NMG Group is a global advisory and intermediary firm focused exclusively on the financial services sector. companySlogan: Vi driver förändring och skapar hållbara lösningar!
descriptionHtml:
Kravanalytiker till B3 Visab Consulting

Vill du jobba i en central roll där din förmåga att förstå behov och omvandla dem till lösningar gör verklig skillnad?

Hos B3 Visab Consulting fokuserar vi på att stödja finansindustrins utveckling i den digitala ekonomin. Med över 40 års erfarenhet inom området har vi byggt långsiktiga relationer med ledande aktörer inom bank och försäkring. Vår erfarenhet och våra seniora konsulter gör oss till en attraktiv partner för både etablerade företag och snabbväxande utmanare.

Om rollen

Som kravanalytiker blir du en nyckelperson i kundens digitala resa. Du kommer att:

  • Identifiera och omvandla affärsbehov till tydliga krav.
  • Säkerställa att lösningar uppfyller prestanda- och säkerhetskrav.
  • Arbeta nära både verksamheten och utvecklingsteam för att skapa verkligt värde.

Hos oss får du möjligheten att utvecklas i en inkluderande miljö där din kompetens tas till vara och dina idéer gör skillnad.

Vi söker dig som:

  • Har minst 3 års erfarenhet som kravanalytiker eller business analyst.
  • Har jobbat inom bank och/eller försäkring.
  • Är kommunikativ, lösningsorienterad och van att samarbeta i tvärfunktionella team.
  • Behärskar svenska och engelska flytande i både tal och skrift.

Därför ska du välja oss:

  • Attraktiv och flexibel lönemodell.
  • Möjlighet att påverka dina arbetstider och arbetsplats.
  • Tillgång till utbildningar och certifieringar för din professionella utveckling.
  • En stark wellness-satsning genom Team B3, som främjar både hälsa och arbetsglädje.

Vill du veta mer?

Ansök direkt eller kontakta Lidia Hagos (lidia.hagos@b3.se) eller Hanna Skoog (+4670-260 12 65).

companyDescription:
Kustom is a fintech company offering one of Europe’s leading checkout solutions. With the most efficient and customizable product on the market, we help merchants tailor their checkout to fit their shoppers’ needs. By gathering innovative and scalable features, Kustom ensures high conversion rates and drives repeat purchases.

Today, Kustom has 24,000 connected merchants with sales in over 170 countries.
companySlogan: Made to convert. A checkout solution tailored to your needs.
descriptionHtml:
Be a part of the next big thing

Do you thrive on tackling complex challenges? Are you passionate about creating impactful solutions that drive commerce forward? Do you believe in encouraging and elevating those around you?

About Us

Kustom, formerly known as Klarna Checkout (KCO), is the Nordic market leader in digital checkout solutions. Today, we support over 24,000 merchants across more than 170 countries, providing a streamlined checkout experience. We offer localized solutions in 18 markets, tailored to specific languages, currencies and payment methods. No matter where businesses operate, we deliver a checkout experience that meets the needs of both merchants and shoppers - driving growth globally. 🌍

The role

We are looking to hire a Commercial Analyst to join our Commercial team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for analyzing customer behavior, monitoring market trends and generating insights that drive our strategic decisions. You'll play a key role in supporting commercial growth, optimizing our approach to pricing, customer segmentation and competitive intelligence.

As part of our team, you'll go beyond just crunching numbers - you’ll translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations. You’ll work cross-functionally to support everything from strategic initiatives to M&A evaluations, helping shape the future direction of our business.

Here at Kustom, you'll collaborate with skilled colleagues across Strategy, Finance, Product and Data to unlock opportunities through data. From building dashboards to performing due diligence, your work will have a direct impact on our growth. Our teams are based in Stockholm, where we value in-person collaboration to strengthen our culture and teamwork.

Things you'll be doing

Customer Behavior Analysis

  • Analyze customer behavior across segments and payment methods
  • Define data requirements and work with the Data team to ensure quality and accessibility
  • Structure data from operational systems for commercial use
  • Build dashboards to support business decision-making

Market & Competitive Analysis

  • Compile macroeconomic and industry reports across current and potential markets
  • Track and summarize competitor activity and trends
  • Monitor market developments using both internal data and external research
  • Maintain a live watchlist of market trends and strategic players

Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A)

  • Support the evaluation of potential acquisition targets
  • Assist in the commercial due diligence process during M&A initiatives

Skills And Experience You'll Need

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance or a related field
  • 1–3 years of experience in an analytical role (outstanding recent graduates are also welcome to apply)
  • Strong analytical mindset and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent Excel skills
  • Familiarity with SQL is a plus (but not a requirement - logical thinkers pick it up quickly)
  • Experience working with BI tools is an advantage
  • Great communication skills and ability to translate data into actionable insights
  • Self-driven, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team

We believe you'll thrive in this role if you have

  • A deep understanding of e-commerce metrics and customer behavior patterns
  • A sharp, analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
  • Excellent communication skills - you know how to make complex data clear for any audience
  • Experience analyzing checkout flows, conversion funnels and payment systems
  • A proactive, self-driven approach - you can take initiative while working closely with others
  • A proven ability to use data to inform and influence business decisions
  • Genuine curiosity and a passion for improving the customer journey through insights and experimentation

Benefits

  • Robust pension plan with national and occupational components
  • Excellent health insurance coverage, including long-term illness protection and salary exchange options
  • Annual wellness allowance of 3,000 SEK to invest in your health and fitness
  • Extra day off when major holidays (e.g., Christmas Eve, New Year's Eve) fall on weekends
  • Continuous learning opportunities and professional development support

A place to grow

At Kustom, you won’t just be another face in the crowd. This is a place where we elevate each other and celebrate success as a team. We believe in our ability to create excellence - and have fun doing it!

We know we set high expectations, but we also know that the road to success isn’t a straight line. We support and encourage each other to try, to fail, to try again. Because that’s how we grow, individually and together.

Does this sound like the place for you?

About Your Application

  • Please apply in English. At Kustom, we welcome everyone! We don’t care where you’re from, whom you love, or what kind of food is your favorite (unless we’re celebrating together, then we’ll need to know!). We believe in inclusivity for all.
  • Given the above, we treat all candidates the same! That’s why we have this awesome recruitment tool to keep track of all the amazing talents that apply - so please; apply through the system and we’ll make sure to keep you updated along the recruitment process. Please note that we can’t keep track of CVs or similar documents sent via email.
  • We value your privacy! When you apply, we'll handle your data with care. Want to know more about how we use your information? Check out our FAQs at the bottom of the jobs page - we've got all the details there for you!

companyDescription:
This is not your typical financial institution. It’s our people who make us a cut above. Here, every person is respected because of their differences, not in spite of them. We pride ourselves on a culture of purpose, passion and compassion.

At Mizuho, we provide the stability of an international industry leader with the career trajectory of a growing business. Our steady, strategic growth gives our people at all levels rewarding degrees of responsibility and a richer work experience than a boutique firm or an established giant could offer alone. 

Working for Mizuho opens doors not just to a rewarding career with excellent prospects, but to lasting friendships with colleagues from diverse cultures. It’s the local expertise of our employees that makes our global network so powerful. By collaborating with colleagues and clients who have your same ambition, you can amplify your sphere of influence and base of knowledge as part of one of the largest—and growing—banks in the world. 

We’re all global citizens, and that’s why our company feels compelled to make an impact through more than just drawing up deals. We prove that it’s possible to do well and do good. We do right by our clients, our community and each other.
descriptionHtml:
Job Description. Analyst, Stockholm

Greenhill: an overview

Greenhill is a global investment bank focused on providing financial advice on significant mergers, acquisitions, restructurings, financings and capital raising to corporations, partnerships, institutions and governments.

We act for clients located throughout the world from our offices in New York, Chicago, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Houston, London, Madrid, Melbourne, Paris, San Francisco, Singapore, Stockholm, Sydney, and Toronto. Since 2023, Greenhill is a part of the Mizuho Financial Group which is one of the largest financial institutions in the world, with 800+ offices worldwide in 40 countries and with more than $1.8 trillion in assets.

Investment Banking Analyst

The Analyst position at Greenhill offers qualified candidates exposure to all aspects of global mergers and acquisitions and corporate restructuring. Due to the size of the Firm, an Analyst position at Greenhill provides outstanding experience and unparalleled exposure to its senior professionals.

Analyst responsibilities include valuing companies using a variety of methodologies, preparing and delivering presentations, conducting research, drafting selling memoranda, analysing business models and participating in due diligence and client meetings.

Key Responsibilities Include

  • Preparation of client presentations analysing financial information relating to specific companies/industries;
  • Financial analysis and financial modelling of companies, including:
  • Valuation across a range of methodologies;
  • Combination and “merger” analysis;
  • Financing and deal structuring;
  • Monitoring of client news flow and general business and industry research;
  • Monitoring the financial news using specialist media sources;
  • Assisting with the project management of client mandates, typically advice to corporates regarding:
  • Mergers, acquisitions and disposals;
  • Private credit financing advisory
  • Financial restructuring;
  • General financial advisory.
  • Supporting and working within live transactions workflow including:
  • Development of potential buyer lists;
  • Supporting preparation of confidential offering memoranda;
  • Preparation of management presentations;
  • Supporting corporate due diligence;
  • Liaising with other professional advisory firms such as accountants and lawyers;
  • Client interaction at multiple levels typically below board level;
  • Other ad-hoc tasks as necessary; and
  • Development training and coaching of analyst interns.

Essential Skills & Experience

  • Exceptional academic credentials with at least a 2:1 or equivalent from a leading university, as well as having:
  • A strong work ethic and the ability to perform well under pressure;
  • A keen eye for detail, strong numerical ability and logical reasoning;
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain complex ideas clearly;
  • Demonstrable commercial awareness and a real interest in transactions; and
  • A strong track record of excellence in extra-curricular activities alongside your studies.
  • Previous internship and/or full-time experience in investment banking at a leading institution is required for this role
  • Fluency in English is required and preferably one Nordic language.

Greenhill: Equality, Diversity and Inclusion

As part of working with internationally-minded clients, often as part of a close cross-border collaboration with other Greenhill offices, fostering diversity of thought throughout the firm is of significant importance to Greenhill. This importance drives a recruitment strategy that seeks to identify candidates based on their capabilities and affinity to a collaboration-based team environment, as well as the cultural value-add they can bring to our firm.

Greenhill & Co., Inc., part of Mizuho Americas, is focused on providing financial advice on significant mergers, acquisitions, restructurings, financings and capital raising to corporations, partnerships, institutions and governments globally. It acts on behalf of clients throughout the world from its offices in the Americas, EMEA, and APAC.
